response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web software developer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang skills, but the role requires deep hardware expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design. Apart from brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and low-latency DLang services at Dunnhumby, there is no evidence of computer architecture, GPU/SIMD, deep-learning workload optimization, or silicon-adjacent experience. The profile does not match the core requirements of a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: 18+ years of software engineering experience, exceeding the years-of-experience bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec with async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and teaching/mentoring track record
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, computer architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, or SIMD/MIMD optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level performance architecture for AI accelerators

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU workload background
Low-latency systems and performance tuning exposure
